course_title: Introduction to Principles of Physiotherapy Assessment and Management

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the roles of anatomy and kinesiology in physiotherapy assessment and management.
Identify key measurement principles in physiotherapy practice and research, and justify their importance in ensuring accuracy.
Effectively perform and explain basic muscle and joint assessment procedures, including manual muscle tests, goniometry, muscle length tests, and passive movements.
Demonstrate client-centred communication strategies to obtain informed consent, deliver client education, and share knowledge with the client and relevant others in a clear and collaborative manner.
Analyse human movement using basic anatomical, physiological, biomechanical, and psychological principles.
Apply problem-solving strategies to foundational clinical situations.
Demonstrate an ethical and professional approach to physiotherapy practice.
Apply ergonomic principles to handle and position clients, ensuring safety, effectiveness, comfort and to minimise risk to the client and the physiotherapist.

assessments.due_date:
Week 5, Fri

The In-semester practical exam will be held during the regular practical times where possible (i.e., students should expect that their exam will be scheduled either during their regular practical time, or as close to it as possible).
assessments.task_description:
Practical examination covering all relevant practical techniques including measurements, manual muscle testing, passive movements and muscle length testing learned for Region 1 - the lower limb.

Students will be required to perform various tests, measurements and/or treatments in a series of practical examinations. The emphasis in these examinations is on the EFFECTIVE and SAFE PERFORMANCE of these procedures, although examiners will also question students about the theory underlying the techniques. The criteria mark sheet for practical examinations is available on Blackboard in the assessment folder.

Practical examinations will be scheduled on-campus in a face-to-face format. 

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Practical Exam - Regions 2 and 3 - Spine & Upper Limb
assessments.task_description:
End of semester practical examination covering material taught for the head, neck and spine and upper limb regions.

Students will be required to perform various tests and measurements in a series of practical assessments. The emphasis in these examinations is on the EFFECTIVE and SAFE PERFORMANCE of these procedures, although examiners will also question students about the theory underlying the techniques. The criteria mark sheet for practical examinations is available on Blackboard in the assessment folder.

Practical examinations will be scheduled on-campus in a face-to-face format, during the central examination period. 

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
End of semester theory exam covering theoretical concepts from all course content (Regions 1-3).

The end of semester theory will be divided into sections, each section may contain one or more multiple choice, short answer, problem solving, or essay-style questions. There will be a focus on clinical reasoning. The theory exam will be a closed book, written exam held on campus.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please see the Assessment tab on Blackboard for information about this assessment item.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Region 1 - lower limb theory

Weekly lectures are provided online, designed for self-paced learning. Weeks 1-5 will include content on assessment and management of lower limb.
learning_activities.topic_description: Region 1 - lower limb theory Weekly lectures are provided online, designed for self-paced learning. Weeks 1-5 will include content on assessment and management of lower limb.

learning_activities.topic:
Region 1 - lower limb practice

Partcipants will attend a 2-hour in-person practical session on campus each week. To prepare, participants must complete online learning and associated preparatory tasks prior to attending. Region 1 will include practical classes on the lower limb.

learning_activities.topic:
Regions 1 - 3 - tutorials

Tutorials will be delivered at scheduled times over the semester (please refer to student timetables for allocated times). Tutorials will include small group activities to improve learning, consolidation of knowledge and clinical reasoning.
